title: Sour Gooey Thai Chicken Curry
description: A lazy Sunday Thai dish featuring sour and gooey flavors, made with cooking wine and poached chicken, perfect for a low-fat friendly meal. This curry is a delicious and easy-to-make dish that is sure to become a favorite.
instructions: In a large pot, combine the chicken, cooking wine, and enough water to cover the chicken. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and poach for 10-12 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Remove the chicken from the pot and set aside. Reserve the poaching liquid.
In a large pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the curry paste and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes, or until fragrant.
Add the fish sauce, lime juice, and brown sugar to the pan. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the coconut milk to the pan and stir to combine. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Add the poached chicken to the pan and stir to combine with the curry sauce.
Add the reserved poaching liquid to the pan, 1/4 cup at a time, until the desired consistency is reached.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the curry for 5-7 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Season the curry with salt to taste.
Stir in the chopped basil leaves.
Serve the curry hot, garnished with additional basil leaves if desired.
